4	from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks

8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":
85	    main()

8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":

72	        else:

## Comprehensive Project Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the Python CLI bookmark manager project. Here's a complete analysis:

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE

```
/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775313251997-3ijucf/

│   ├── storage.py (persistence layer - 66 lines)
│   └── formatter.py (output formatting - 45 lines)
├── tests/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── test_storage.py (117 lines)
│   └── test_search.py (180 lines - defines search tests but src/search.py doesn't exist yet)
└── [Python cache files and egg-info directories]
```

### 2. DATA MODEL (src/models.py)

The `Bookmark` dataclass has this structure:
- **id**: str (UUID format)
- **url**: str (the bookmarked URL)
- **title**: str (display name)
- **tags**: List[str] (optional, defaults to empty list)
- **notes**: str (optional, defaults to empty string)
- **created_at**: str (ISO format datetime, auto-set on creation)

Methods:
- `to_dict()` - serializes to dictionary
- `from_dict(data)` - deserializes from dictionary

### 3. EXISTING COMMANDS (src/cli.py)

The CLI currently supports 4 commands via argparse:

**add** - Adds a new bookmark
```
bookmark add <url> <title> [--tags TAG1 TAG2 ...] [--notes "notes"]
```
- Creates a UUID id
- Returns confirmation with id

**list** - Lists all bookmarks
```
bookmark list [--format {table|json}]
```
- Default format: table
- Supports JSON output

**delete** - Deletes a bookmark by ID
```
bookmark delete <id>
```
- Returns success/failure message

**show** - Shows a single bookmark
```
bookmark show <id>
```
- Displays one bookmark in table format
- Returns not found error if ID doesn't exist

### 4. STORAGE LAYER (src/storage.py)

- **Default path**: `~/.bookmarks.json`
- **Format**: JSON array of bookmark objects
- **Key functions**:
  - `load_bookmarks(db_path)` - loads all bookmarks from JSON
  - `save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)` - writes all bookmarks to JSON
  - `add_bookmark(url, title, tags, notes, db_path)` - creates and persists
  - `delete_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)` - removes by ID
  - `get_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)` - retrieves single bookmark
- All functions accept optional `db_path` parameter for testing

### 5. OUTPUT FORMATTING (src/formatter.py)

**format_table(bookmarks)** - Plain-text table output
- Column widths: ID=8, Title=30, URL=40, Tags=20
- Uses first 8 chars of UUID as display ID
- Truncates long values to column widths
- Returns "No bookmarks found." for empty list
- Returns formatted string with header, separator, and rows

**format_json(bookmarks)** - JSON output
- Uses `Bookmark.to_dict()` for each bookmark
- Pretty-printed with 2-space indentation

### 6. ARGUMENT PARSING PATTERNS

All subparsers use `add_subparsers(dest="command")` to identify the command.
- Arguments parsed with positional args and optional flags
- `--tags` uses `nargs="*"` to accept multiple values
- `--format` uses `choices` to restrict to valid options
- `--notes` is a simple string argument

### 7. EXISTING TESTS

**tests/test_storage.py** (117 lines)
- Tests for all storage functions
- Uses pytest fixtures for db_path and sample_bookmarks
- Covers: loading, saving, adding, deleting, retrieving
- Tests edge cases (missing files, nonexistent IDs)

**tests/test_search.py** (180 lines) - **IMPORTANT: Tests for functionality that doesn't exist yet**
This file defines the requirements for a `search` module that needs to be implemented:

**Functions to implement in src/search.py:**
- `search_by_title(bookmarks, query)` - case-insensitive substring match
- `search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag)` - exact tag matching
- `search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain)` - URL domain matching (including subdomains)
- `search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None)` - combined search with AND logic

**Test expectations:**
- `search_by_title("python")` should match "Python Tutorial" and "RunPod Python SDK"
- `search_by_tag("docs")` should match 3 bookmarks with docs tag
- `search_by_domain("github.com")` should match 2 bookmarks
- `search_by_domain("docs.python.org")` should match 2 bookmarks (subdomain logic)
- `search()` with no args returns all bookmarks
- Multiple criteria use AND logic: `search(title="python", tag="sdk")` returns only bookmarks matching both
- No match returns empty list

**CLI integration expectations (from tests):**
- New `search` command should accept: `--title`, `--tag`, `--domain`, `--format` flags
- Should work with both "table" and "json" formats
- Parser should accept: `search --title python`, `search --tag docs --format json`

### 8. KEY PROJECT DETAILS

- **Python requirement**: >=3.10
- **Dependencies**: pytest>=7.4.0 (for testing)
- **Entry point**: `bookmark` command maps to `src.cli:main`
- **Database format**: JSON array stored in `~/.bookmarks.json`
- **ID generation**: UUID v4 converted to string

### 9. COMMAND FLOW PATTERNS

1. **Argument parsing**: `build_parser()` creates all subparsers and options
2. **Command dispatch**: `main()` checks `args.command` and routes to appropriate handler
3. **Storage operations**: Each command imports and calls storage functions
4. **Output**: Results are formatted via `format_table()` or `format_json()` then printed
5. **Error handling**: Uses `sys.exit(1)` for errors, prints error messages to stdout

### 10. SAMPLE TEST DATA

Test fixtures provide realistic bookmark examples with:
- Python documentation URLs
- GitHub repository URLs
- Mozilla Developer Network links
- Tags: python, tutorial, docs, sdk, javascript, flask, cli, runpod, mdn, web
- Notes field for additional context
- ISO datetime created_at values

---

**Key Gap to Implement**: The test file `test_search.py` expects a `src/search.py` module with four search functions and CLI integration in `src/cli.py` that doesn't yet exist. This is the feature that needs to be added based on the comprehensive test specifications provided.

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

# Plan: Add Search Functionality to Bookmark CLI

## Context
The bookmark CLI tool (`src/cli.py`) currently supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to find bookmarks by title, tag, or domain. `tests/test_search.py` already exists (180 lines) and fully specifies the expected behavior — all tests currently fail because `src/search.py` doesn't exist and the CLI has no `search` command. This plan implements exactly what those tests require.

---

## Files to Modify / Create

| Action | File |
|--------|------|
| **Create** | `src/search.py` |
| **Modify** | `src/cli.py` |

---

## Step 1 — Create `src/search.py`

Use `urllib.parse.urlparse` (stdlib) to extract the netloc (hostname) from URLs for domain matching. No new dependencies needed.

```python
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import List, Optional
from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Case-insensitive substring match on title."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match against the bookmark's tags list."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Match if URL's netloc equals domain OR is a subdomain of domain."""
    results = []
    for b in bookmarks:
        netloc = urlparse(b.url).netloc
        if netloc == domain or netloc.endswith("." + domain):
            results.append(b)
    return results


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Combine any/all criteria with AND logic. No criteria → return all."""
    result = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        result = search_by_title(result, title)
    if tag is not None:
        result = search_by_tag(result, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        result = search_by_domain(result, domain)
    return result
```

**Domain matching rationale:**
- `search_by_domain(bookmarks, "github.com")` → netloc `"github.com"` == `"github.com"` ✓ (2 matches)
- `search_by_domain(bookmarks, "docs.python.org")` → netloc `"docs.python.org"` == `"docs.python.org"` ✓ (2 matches)
- Subdomain logic (`endswith("." + domain)`) also handles cases like querying `"python.org"` matching `"docs.python.org"`.

---

## Step 2 — Modify `src/cli.py`

### 2a. Add import at top of file (after existing imports)
```python
from .search import search
```

### 2b. Add `search` subparser inside `build_parser()`, after the `show` block (line 39, before `return parser`)
```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Search by title (substring)")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Search by tag (exact)")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Search by URL domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

### 2c. Add `search` handler inside `main()`, after the `show` block (before the final `if __name__` line)
```python
elif args.command == "search":
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

---

## Verification

Run all tests:
```bash
pytest tests/ -v
```

Expected outcome:
- `tests/test_storage.py` — all previously passing tests continue to pass (no changes to storage)
- `tests/test_search.py` — all 14 tests pass:
  - `TestSearchByTitle` (3 tests)
  - `TestSearchByTag` (3 tests)
  - `TestSearchByDomain` (3 tests)
  - `TestSearchCombined` (7 tests)
  - `TestSearchCLI` (2 tests)

Manual smoke test:
```bash
bookmark search --title python
bookmark search --tag docs --format json
bookmark search --title flask --domain github.com
```

# Plan: Add Search Functionality to Bookmark CLI

## Context
The bookmark CLI tool (`src/cli.py`) currently supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to find bookmarks by title, tag, or domain. `tests/test_search.py` already exists (180 lines) and fully specifies the expected behavior — all tests currently fail because `src/search.py` doesn't exist and the CLI has no `search` command. This plan implements exactly what those tests require.

---

## Files to Modify / Create

| Action | File |
|--------|------|
| **Create** | `src/search.py` |
| **Modify** | `src/cli.py` |

---

## Step 1 — Create `src/search.py`

Use `urllib.parse.urlparse` (stdlib) to extract the netloc (hostname) from URLs for domain matching. No new dependencies needed.

```python
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import List, Optional
from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Case-insensitive substring match on title."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match against the bookmark's tags list."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Match if URL's netloc equals domain OR is a subdomain of domain."""
    results = []
    for b in bookmarks:
        netloc = urlparse(b.url).netloc
        if netloc == domain or netloc.endswith("." + domain):
            results.append(b)
    return results


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Combine any/all criteria with AND logic. No criteria → return all."""
    result = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        result = search_by_title(result, title)
    if tag is not None:
        result = search_by_tag(result, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        result = search_by_domain(result, domain)
    return result
```

**Domain matching rationale:**
- `search_by_domain(bookmarks, "github.com")` → netloc `"github.com"` == `"github.com"` ✓ (2 matches)
- `search_by_domain(bookmarks, "docs.python.org")` → netloc `"docs.python.org"` == `"docs.python.org"` ✓ (2 matches)
- Subdomain logic (`endswith("." + domain)`) also handles cases like querying `"python.org"` matching `"docs.python.org"`.

---

```

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose title contains query (case-insensitive substring match)."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks that have an exact match for the given tag."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose URL domain matches the given domain exactly."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks matching all provided criteria (AND logic).

    If no criteria are given, all bookmarks are returned.
    """
    results = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results

def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,

        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")

            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json
from .search import search


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    # --- search ---
    search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
    search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Search by title (case-insensitive substring)")
    search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Search by tag (exact match)")
    search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Search by URL domain")
    search_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))

    elif args.command == "search":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(results))
        else:
            print(format_table(results))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
